title: eriogonum atrorubens var. rupestre
intro: Eriogonum atrorubens var. rupestre, also known as red-stemmed buckwheat or mountain buckwheat, is a variety of flowering plant in the buckwheat family, native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum atrorubens var. rupestre include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to support overall health and well-being.
